# VSTest → Microsoft.Testing.Platform Migration Plan

## Summary

Migrate 5 MSTest 3.8.0 test projects from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform (MTP). MSTest 3.8.0 already supports MTP (requires ≥3.2.0), so no framework version upgrade is needed. The .NET 8 SDK requires the compatibility shim (`TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport`) and `--` separator for MTP args.

---

## Prerequisites

- All 5 test projects use MSTest 3.8.0 ✓ (≥3.2.0 required)
- .NET 8 SDK ✓ (will use VSTest-bridge mode for `dotnet test`)

---

## Todos

### 1. Establish baseline
- Run `dotnet test` across all 5 projects, record pass/fail counts
- Document current Azure DevOps pipeline arguments (filters, TRX paths, coverage settings)

### 2. Update Directory.Build.props
Add the following properties (adjust the condition to match your test project naming convention):

```xml
<PropertyGroup Condition="$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
  <!-- Enable MTP runner for MSTest -->
  <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
  <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>

  <!-- .NET 8 SDK compatibility: enables dotnet test to drive MTP projects -->
  <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
</PropertyGroup>
```

> ⚠️ Do NOT use `Condition="'$(IsTestProject)' == 'true'"` — it's not defined when Directory.Build.props is evaluated.

### 3. Add MTP extension NuGet packages
Add to each test project (or via Directory.Build.props / Directory.Packages.props):

```xml
<!-- TRX reporting (replaces --logger trx) -->
<PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.TrxReport" Version="1.6.2" />

<!-- Code coverage (replaces --collect "Code Coverage") -->
<PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.CodeCoverage" Version="17.13.0" />
```

### 4. Update Azure DevOps pipeline

Replace the `VSTest@3` task with `DotNetCoreCLI@2`:

```yaml
# BEFORE
- task: VSTest@3
  inputs:
    testAssemblyVer2: '**/*Tests.dll'
    runSettingsFile: 'test.runsettings'
    codeCoverageEnabled: true

# AFTER
- task: DotNetCoreCLI@2
  displayName: 'Run Tests'
  inputs:
    command: 'test'
    projects: '**/*Tests.csproj'
    arguments: >-
      --no-build --configuration Release
      -- --report-trx --results-directory $(Agent.TempDirectory)
      --coverage --coverage-output-format cobertura
```

Key changes:
| VSTest argument | MTP equivalent |
|---|---|
| `--logger trx` | `--report-trx` (after `--`) |
| `--collect "Code Coverage"` | `--coverage --coverage-output-format cobertura` (after `--`) |
| `--filter <expr>` | `--filter <expr>` (same syntax, but after `--`) |
| `--settings <file>` | `--settings <file>` (after `--`) |
| `--blame-hang` | `--hangdump` (needs HangDump package) |
| `--blame-crash` | `--crashdump` (needs CrashDump package) |

> ⚠️ On .NET 8 SDK, MTP arguments go **after** `--`. Build args (`--no-build`, `-c`) stay before.

### 5. Publish test results (if TRX was auto-published by VSTest@3)

`VSTest@3` auto-publishes results. With `DotNetCoreCLI@2`, add a publish step if needed:

```yaml
- task: PublishTestResults@2
  displayName: 'Publish Test Results'
  inputs:
    testResultsFormat: 'VSTest'
    testResultsFiles: '$(Agent.TempDirectory)/**/*.trx'
  condition: always()
```

### 6. Handle exit code 8 (zero tests discovered)

MTP returns exit code 8 when no tests are found (VSTest returned 0). If any project conditionally discovers zero tests, either:
- Fix discovery, or
- Add to Directory.Build.props:
```xml
<TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>$(TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments) --ignore-exit-code 8</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>
```

### 7. Remove VSTest-only packages (after verification)

Once confirmed working, remove from all test projects:
- `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` — not needed for MTP
- `MSTest.TestAdapter` — not needed for MTP (the runner is built-in)

### 8. Verify
- [ ] `dotnet build` — zero errors
- [ ] `dotnet test` — all tests pass, counts match baseline
- [ ] Run test exe directly: `./bin/Debug/net8.0/MyProject.Tests.exe`
- [ ] CI pipeline produces TRX files and code coverage
- [ ] No `vstest.console.exe` or `VSTest@3` references remain

---

## Common Pitfalls

| Pitfall | Solution |
|---------|----------|
| MTP args ignored | Must use `--` separator on .NET 8 SDK |
| Exit code 8 in CI | Zero tests discovered — use `--ignore-exit-code 8` or fix discovery |
| Mixed VSTest/MTP projects | Unsupported — migrate all 5 projects together |
| `IsTestProject` condition | Not available in Directory.Build.props — use name-based condition |

---

## Future: Upgrade to .NET 10 SDK

When you upgrade to .NET 10+, you can drop the compatibility shim and use native MTP mode:
1. Remove `<TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>`
2. Add to `global.json`: `"test": { "runner": "Microsoft.Testing.Platform" }`
3. Remove the `--` separator from all `dotnet test` invocations

---

## References

- [Migrate from VSTest to MTP](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/core/testing/migrating-vstest-microsoft-testing-platform)
- [Microsoft.Testing.Platform overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/core/testing/microsoft-testing-platform-intro)
- [MTP CLI options](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/core/testing/microsoft-testing-platform-cli-options)
